focus: Driver runner block — runner-mandatory gating + B3 strip-then-append + {PROMPT} substitution + cross-runtime parity
harness: evals/run_evals.py — sets up a sandbox git repo per scenario, points runner.command at evals/mock-agent.py, runs the rendered driver template with max-iter=1, compares the mock's argv_dump.json against expected.

evals.name: missing-runner-aborts
evals.prompt: prd.json contains valid project/branchName/description/userStories but NO runner key. Driver must abort before iter 1.
assertions: argv_dump.json was NOT created (mock never spawned)

evals.name: passthrough-no-cli-model
evals.prompt: runner.args has --model opus, no CLI --model given. argv passes through verbatim, no strip.

evals.name: strip-long-form
evals.prompt: runner.args has '--model opus', CLI --model sonnet. Driver strips 'opus' pair, appends '--model sonnet' once.

evals.name: strip-equals-form
evals.prompt: runner.args has '--model=opus', CLI --model sonnet. Driver strips the = token, appends '--model sonnet'.

evals.name: strip-short-form
evals.prompt: runner.args has '-m opus', CLI --model sonnet. Driver strips the pair, appends '--model sonnet'.

evals.name: strip-equals-short
evals.prompt: runner.args has '-m=opus', CLI --model sonnet. Driver strips the token, appends '--model sonnet'.

evals.name: strip-duplicates
evals.prompt: runner.args has '--model a' AND '--model b' (two model flags), CLI --model c. Both stripped, c appended once.
assertions: argv contains neither 'a' nor 'b' as model values

evals.name: dangling-aborts
evals.prompt: runner.args has '--model' at the very end (no value), CLI --model X given. Driver must abort the strip step.

evals.name: prompt-substitution
evals.prompt: {PROMPT} sentinel in runner.args is replaced at runtime with .ralph/prompt.md content.
assertions: argv element corresponding to {PROMPT} equals 'SENTINEL-CONTENT-A1B2C3\n' EXACTLY (trailing newline preserved)

evals.name: prompt-missing-sentinel
evals.prompt: runner.args lacks {PROMPT}. Driver appends prompt content at end and emits a stderr warning.
assertions: stderr contains warning about missing '{PROMPT}' sentinel

evals.name: prompt-trailing-newline-preserved
evals.prompt: Prompt content has a trailing newline. After {PROMPT} substitution, the substituted argv element must equal the file content byte-for-byte, including the trailing \n.
assertions: the argv element substituted from {PROMPT} equals 'hello-world\n' exactly (length 12, ends with 0x0A)

evals.name: embedded-newline-in-args-preserved
evals.prompt: runner.args has an element with an embedded \n. After driver resolution, that element remains a SINGLE argv entry containing the newline — not split into two entries. {PROMPT} sentinel included so the prompt-append fallback doesn't contaminate the count.
assertions: argv has exactly 4 elements (the mock receives -p, prompt, --note, multi-line value)
argv element 3 equals 'line1\nline2' (single arg containing the newline)
